zotero.abstractNote: We present Neural Localizer Fields (NLF), a novel approach for continuous 3D human pose and shape estimation from monocular video. Existing methods typically rely on frame-by-frame estimation or recurrent architectures, which often struggle with temporal consistency and occlusion. In contrast, NLF represents the human body as a continuous field of localizers that predict the 3D position of body joints and surface vertices. By leveraging a continuous representation, NLF can be queried at any time point, enabling smooth and temporally consistent motion estimation. We demonstrate that NLF achieves state-of-the-art performance on standard benchmarks while providing a more robust and flexible framework for handling challenging scenarios such as fast motion and self-occlusion.
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems 38
